title_summary: GET /auth/me with a malformed/invalid token is rejected (401/403)
description: When I exercise the 'GET /auth/me with a malformed/invalid token is rejected (401/403)' check against GET /auth/me on http://localhost:8899, I expect status in [401, 403]; body has 'message'. but I get: HTTP 500. message='invalid signature' FAILED: status in [401, 403].
steps_to_reproduce: 1. Send GET /auth/me with a malformed Bearer token.
expected_result: status in [401, 403]; body has 'message'. (Per the documentation (data/documentation-reviewer/reference/auth.md:9 — https://dummyjson.com/docs/auth): "When the Authorization header is missing or the token is invalid, the API returns 401 Unauthorized.")
actual_result: HTTP 500. message='invalid signature' FAILED: status in [401, 403].
severity_impact: Blocker — crash, data loss, or auth/data exposure

documentation.text: When the Authorization header is missing or the token is invalid, the API returns 401 Unauthorized.
documentation.reviewer_reason: The documentation states that GET /auth/me with an invalid token should return 401 Unauthorized, but the observed response is HTTP 500. The actual status code (500) contradicts the documented expected behavior (401).

documented_expected: Per the documentation (data/documentation-reviewer/reference/auth.md:9 — https://dummyjson.com/docs/auth): "When the Authorization header is missing or the token is invalid, the API returns 401 Unauthorized."
